steps.title: Use isosceles geometry to set up a right triangle
steps.content: Triangle JKL is isosceles with JK = KL and base JL = 60. When we drop a perpendicular from apex K to the base JL, it bisects the base at point M. Therefore, JM = 30.

steps.title: Apply the tangent ratio to find the height
steps.content:
In right triangle JKM, the angle at J has a tangent ratio of opposite over adjacent:
$$\tan J = \frac{KM}{JM}$$
Substituting $\tan J = \frac{3}{10}$ and $JM = 30$:
$$\frac{KM}{30} = \frac{3}{10}$$
Therefore, KM = 9 units.

steps.title: Calculate the area using base and height
steps.content:
The area of the triangle is:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 60 \times 9 = 270$$
B is correct.
